videoTitle: Lesson 3.10- Campaign Planning and Execution Checklist.mp4
transcript: Welcome to Lesson 3.6, Campaign Planning and Execution Checklist. This is the final lesson in Module 3 and it brings together everything you have learned with a practical, systematic framework. You have learned about inbound marketing, outbound marketing, when to use each strategy, how to build integrated campaigns and specific lead generation tactics. Now, you need a checklist that ensures you execute campaigns systematically without missing critical steps. By the end of this lesson, you will have a complete campaign planning and execution framework covering pre-campaign planning, the build phase, the launch phase, and ongoing monitoring and optimization. You will also learn common pitfalls and how to avoid them. This checklist-based approach is what separates professional campaign execution from amateur efforts. Let's dive in. Every successful campaign moves through four distinct phases. Understanding these phases helps you stay organized and ensures nothing falls through the cracks. Phase 1 is Plan. This typically takes week 1 to 2. This is where you do research, develop strategy, and set up foundations. You are building your blueprint before you build anything. Phase 2 is build. This typically takes week 2 to 3. This is where you create your assets, build your landing pages, set up automation, and prepare everything for launch. You are executing based on your plan. Phase 3 is launch. This typically happens in week 4. This is where you test everything thoroughly, deploy your campaign, and go live. You are making your campaign public. Phase 4 is optimized. This is ongoing after launch. This is where you monitor performance, test variations, and continuously improve. You are making your campaign better over time. Each phase has its own specific checklist to ensure nothing is missed. The checklists build on each other. You cannot skip phase 1 and jump to phase 3. Each phase depends on the previous phase being completed properly. Let me walk you through each phase and its checklist. Phase 1 is pre-campaign planning. This is your foundation. If you skip items here, your campaign will struggle later. Section A is strategy clarity. First, check that your campaign goal is defined specifically and measurably. Not get more leads, but generate 50 qualified leads in 30 days. Second, check that your target audience persona is documented. You know who you are targeting. Third, check that success metrics and KPIs are identified. You know what numbers matter. Fourth, check that timeline and deadlines are established. Everyone knows when things need to happen. Section B is channel selection. First, check that channels are selected based on actual audience research, not assumptions. Second, check that you have balanced inbound and outbound channels. Third, check that budget is allocated across channels with percentages assigned. Fourth, check that your integration strategy is chosen. Are you using awareness amplification, retargeting reinforcement, nurture acceleration, or multi-touch journey? Section C is message development. First, check that your core message is crafted. Your fundamental value proposition is clear. Second, check that channel-specific adaptations are planned. You know how the message adapts to each format. Third, check the call to actions are defined for each channel. You know what action you want to people take. Fourth, check that your messaging matrix is completed to ensure consistency. Section D is resource preparation. First, check that team roles and responsibilities are assigned. Everyone knows their job. Second check that you have access to all tools and platforms needed Third check that budget is approved and actually available Fourth check that a content creation timeline exists You know when each asset needs to be completed. Complete every item in phase 1 before moving to phase 2. Strong planning prevents poor performance. Phase 2 is campaign build. This is where you create everything your campaign needs to function. Section A is lead magnets and content. First, check that your lead magnet is created and finalized, not just planned. Second, check that blog posts or content pieces are written completely. Third, check that social media content is prepared and ready to schedule. Fourth, check that email sequences are drafted and reviewed for quality. Fifth, check that ad copy and creative are designed and approved. Section B is landing pages. First, check that landing pages are built for each offer in your campaign. Second, check that headlines and copy are optimized using the formulas from previous lessons. Third, check that the forms are configured asking only for essential information like name and email. Fourth, check that thank you pages are created confirming delivery. Fifth, check that mobile responsiveness is verified. View your pages on actual mobile devices Section C is Automation Setup First, check that CRM workflows are built in GoHighLevel or your chosen platform. Second, check that email sequences are loaded into the system and triggered correctly. Third, check that SMS messages are configured if you are using SMS. Fourth, check that tagging and segmentation rules are set to leads are organized properly. Fifth, check that lead scoring is configured to identify hot prospects. Section D is tracking implementation. First, check that Google Analytics is connected to your website. Second, check that Facebook Pixel is installed if running Facebook ads. Third, check that UTM parameters are created for all links so you can track resources. Fourth, check that conversion tracking is set up to measure goal completions. Fifth, check that CRM reporting dashboards are configured to visualize data. Build once, build right. Cutting corners here costs you later. Testing comes next. Phase 3 is launch. This is where you test everything and deploy your campaign live. The left column is pre-launch testing. Never skip this. First, submit a test form on each landing page to verify it works. Second, verify you received the lead magnet delivery email Check your inbox Third, check that automation workflows trigger correctly when you submit the test form Fourth, test all links to ensure none are broken Click every single link Fifth, view all pages on both mobile and desktop devices Check that everything displays correctly Sixth, verify tracking pixels fire correctly using browser extensions or platform tools Seventh, check that email sequences display properly across different email clients Eighth, test SMS delivery if you are using SMS in your campaign Send yourself a test message Ninth, review all content one final time for typos and errors Fresh eyes catch mistakes The right column is Go Live Deployment. First, submit your ads for platform approval. Facebook and Google review ads before they run. Second, launch or schedule your ad campaigns to start. Third, schedule your social media posts using your chosen scheduling tool. Fourth, publish your blog content and make it live. Fifth, activate your email campaigns in your system. Sixth, turn automation workflows on. They were off during testing. Now, they go live. Seven, notify your team that the campaign is live. Everyone should know. Eight, inform your client that launch has happened. Set expectations for when they will see results. Nine establish your monitoring schedule Who checks what and when At the bottom a warning never launch without testing broken elements cost you money and damage your reputation fix issues before they go live phase 4 is monitor and optimize this is ongoing after launch and it is where good campaigns become great campaigns daily monitoring is critical in the first week check for technical issues or errors. Things break. Catch them fast. Monitor ad spend and pacing. Are you spending too fast or too slow? Review lead volume and quality. Are you getting leads? Are they qualified? Respond to comments and messages promptly. Engagement matters. Check that form submissions are working. Verify data is flowing into your CRM After the first week, shift to weekly reviews Analyze key metrics including traffic, conversions, and cost per lead Compare actual performance to your goals Are you on track? Identify underperforming elements Which ads, which emails, which pages are weak Implement small optimizations Change headlines Adjust targeting Tweak copy Review lead quality and qualification rates Are you attracting the right people? Adjust the budget allocation if needed Move money from weak performers to strong performers Monthly, do strategic optimization Run comprehensive performance analysis across all channels A-B test new major variations like completely different ad creative or landing page designs. Review and update messaging if market conditions change. Analyze attribution data to understand which touchpoints contribute most. Create client or stakeholder reports showing results and recommendations. Plan next month's optimizations based on what you learn. The key principle. Campaigns are never done. They require continuous attention and improvement. The first week is critical, so monitor daily. Then, shift to a weekly rhythm with monthly strategic reviews. Let me show you exactly what to track in your campaign performance dashboard. Metric 1. Reach or Impressions This tells you how many people saw your campaign. Track this by channel. Are people actually seeing your ads and content? Metric 2. Click-through Rate What percentage of people who saw your message clicked to your landing page? For ads, 2 to 5 percent is typical. Below 1 percent means your message or creative needs work. Metric 3. Lead conversion rate. What percentage of landing page visitors became leads? For lead magnets, 15 to 30 percent is good. Below 10 percent That means your landing page or offer needs optimization. Metric 4. Cost per lead. How much are you spending to acquire one lead? Divide your total spend by leads generated. This must be significantly lower than your customer lifetime value or you lose money. Metric 5. Lead quality. What percentage of your leads are actually qualified using your Bant F criteria? 100 unqualified leads are worthless. Track quality, not just quantity. Metric 6. Campaign ROI What is the return on investment? Revenue generated divided by campaign cost. Minimum twice for profitability. Create campaigns achieve 5x to 10x. These six metrics give you complete visibility into campaign performance. Track them in your CRM dashboard and review them weekly. Let me warn you about 5 common campaign pitfalls so you can avoid them. Pitfall number 1. Launching without testing. The problem is broken forms, dead links, and wasted budget on campaigns that do not work. The impact is immediate failure and money down the drain. The fix is simple. Always test everything before going live. Submit test forms, click every link, verify automation triggers. Pitfall 2 No clear success criteria The problem is you launch a campaign but have no idea what success looks like The impact is you cannot tell if the campaign worked or if you should continue The fix Define specific metrics and targets up front in your planning phase. Pitfall 3. Set it and forget it. The problem is launching a campaign and never looking at it again. The impact is performance degrades over time. Opportunities for optimization are missed, and you waste money. The fix? Weekly monitoring minimum, monthly optimization. Campaigns require ongoing attention. Pitfall 4. Ignoring lead quality The problem is focusing only on lead quantity and not quality. The impact is high volume but low conversions, which wastes sales team time. The Fix, track lead to customer rate by channel and adjust targeting to improve quality. Pitfall 5, Giving Up Too Soon The problem is running a campaign for one week, not seeing immediate results and quitting. The impact is you never give the campaign time to work or test variations. The Fix, give campaigns a minimum of 30 days. Test multiple variations, optimize based on data before deciding to stop. Most campaign failures are preventable with proper process. Use your checklist religiously. Let's recap what you learned in this lesson. You learned that campaign success requires systematic execution through four phases. Plan, build, launch, and optimize. Each phase has specific deliverables and checklists. You learned that pre-campaign planning includes defining specific goals, selecting appropriate channels, developing consistent messaging, and preparing all resources before building anything. You learned that build phase covers creating lead magnets and content, building optimized landing pages, setting up automation workflows, and implementing tracking across all channels. You learned that the launch phase requires thorough testing of all elements before deployment, including forms, links, automation, tracking, and mobile display, then systematic go-live process. You'll learn that ongoing monitoring uses daily check-ins in the first week to catch issues, then shifts to weekly reviews of metrics and performance with monthly strategic optimization sessions. You'll learn the key metrics to track, reach and impressions, click-through rate, lead conversion rate, Cost per lead, lead quality percentage, and campaign ROI. You learned that the campaign planning and execution checklist ensures no critical steps are missed throughout the campaign lifecycle. And you learned that common pitfalls include launching without testing, having no success criteria, set it and forget it mentality, ignoring lead quality, and giving up too soon. You now have a systematic process for planning and executing any marketing campaign from start to finish. You can manage campaigns professionally from conception through optimization. Congratulations! You have completed all six lessons in Module 3, Understanding Marketing Campaigns. You learned about inbound marketing and its three pillars of content, SEO and social media. You learned about outbound marketing including ads, cold email, and SMS. You learned how to choose the right strategy based on business stage, budget, timeline, audience behavior, and product type. You learned how to build integrated campaigns that combine inbound and outbound for maximum impact. You learned specific lead generation tactics for capturing leads through multiple channels. And you learned the systematic campaign planning and execution framework. Next up is the module 3 hands-on practice. This is where you will apply everything from all six lessons to design a complete marketing campaign for a fictional business scenario. You will use the templates and frameworks from across the module to create a comprehensive campaign plan that you can add to your portfolio. This hands-on practice synthesizes everything you have learned and gives you real-world experience planning campaigns like a professional marketing automation specialist. Let's apply everything in the module 3 hands-on practice.

visualDescriptions.description:
The image displays a slide from a presentation on campaign planning and execution. 

*   The title of the slide is "Campaign Planning and Execution Checklist" in large black text, with the subtitle "Your step-by-step framework for launching successful campaigns" in smaller gray text underneath.
*   Below the title, there is a green vertical line separating the title from the rest of the content. 
*   To the left of the line, it says "WHAT YOU'LL LEARN" in green text, followed by a list of bullet points describing what will be learned:
    *   Complete pre-campaign planning checklist to ensure solid foundation
    *   Campaign road map phases checklist for efficient execution
    *   Launch phase checklist to help you avoid false starts
    *   Monitoring and optimization checklist for ongoing improvement
    *   Common pitfalls and how to avoid campaign failures
*   On the right side of the slide, there is a flowchart with four steps:
    *   A. Plan
    *   B. Build
    *   C. Launch
    *   D. Optimize
*   In the top-right corner, there is a logo that says "LIBRE" in green text.
*   In the bottom-left corner, it says "Estimated time: 15 minutes" in small gray text.

Overall, the slide provides an overview of the key takeaways from the lesson on campaign planning and execution, including the importance of pre-campaign planning, campaign road mapping, launch phase planning, monitoring and optimization, and avoiding common pitfalls. The flowchart on the right side of the slide illustrates the four key steps involved in campaign planning and execution.

The image presents a comprehensive overview of Phase 1: Pre-Campaign Planning, emphasizing the importance of establishing a solid foundation for campaign success. The title, "Phase 1: Pre-Campaign Planning," is prominently displayed at the top, accompanied by the subtitle "Establish a solid foundation for campaign success." Below this, four main sections are outlined, each with a distinct color and icon.

*   **Strategy Clarity**
    *   Campaign goal defined (specific, measurable)
    *   Target audience persona documented
    *   Success metrics & KPIs identified
    *   Timeline and deadlines established
*   **Channel Selection**
    *   Channels selected based on audience research
    *   Reach and outbound balance determined
    *   Budget allocated across channels
    *   Integration strategy chosen
*   **Message Development**
    *   Core message refined & crafted
    *   Channel-specific adaptations planned
    *   Call-to-action defined per channel
    *   Messaging matrix compiled
*   **Resource Preparation**
    *   Team roles & responsibilities assigned
    *   Tools and platforms access confirmed
    *   Budget approved and available
    *   Content created/stripe created

At the bottom of the image, a black button with white text reads, "Strong planning prevents poor performance." The background of the image is white, providing a clean and professional backdrop for the information presented. Overall, the image effectively communicates the key elements of Phase 1: Pre-Campaign Planning, highlighting the importance of careful planning in achieving campaign success.

The image shows a checklist for Phase 2 of a campaign build, titled **Phase 2: Campaign Build Checklist** with the subtitle **Creating and configuring your campaign assets**.

The checklist is divided into four sections:

* **Lead Magnets & Content** 
	+ Lead magnets created and finalized
	+ Blog posts and core content written
	* Social media content prepared
	* Email sequences written and reviewed
	* Ads copy and creative designed
* **Landing Pages** 
	+ Landing pages built for each offer
	* Headlines and copy optimized
	* *Meta information created and verified*
	* *There you pages created*
	* Mobile responsiveness verified
* **Automation Setup** 
	+ CRM workflows built in Google Ads
	* Email sequences linked to lead system
	* SMS sequences configured
	* Tags and segmentation used
	+ Lead scoring configured
* **Tracking Implementation** 
	+ Google analytics connected
	* Facebook pixel installed
	* UTM parameters created for all links
	* Conversion tracking set up
	+ CRM reporting dashboards configured

At the bottom of the image, a button reads **Build once, build it right - testing comes next**. The background is white with a **LIBRE** logo displayed in the top-right corner.

The image displays a checklist for Phase 2 of a campaign build, titled "Phase 2: Campaign Build Checklist" with a subtitle of "Creating and configuring your campaign assets". 

The checklist is divided into four sections: 
* **Lead Magnets & Content** (purple) 
* **Landing Pages** (blue) 
* **Automation Setup** (orange) 
* **Tracking Implementation** (green)

Each section contains a list of tasks to be completed, represented by checkboxes. The tasks are as follows:

**Lead Magnets & Content:**
- Lead magnets created and finalized
- Blog posts and all content written
- Social media content prepared
- Email sequences written and reviewed
- Ads copy and creative designed

**Landing Pages:**
- Landing pages built for each offer
- Headlines and copy optimized
- Info confirmed to track and send to
- Thank you pages created
- Mobile responsiveness verified

**Automation Setup:**
- CRM workflows built in ClickFunnels
- Email sequences loaded into the system
- SMS sequences configured
- Tags and parameters are set up
- Lead scoring configured

**Tracking Implementation:**
- Google Analytics connected
- Facebook Pixel installed
- UTM parameters created for all links
- Conversion tracking set up
- CRM reporting dashboards configured

At the bottom of the image, there is a black button with white text that reads "Build once, build it right - testing comes next". In the top-right corner, there is a logo that says "LIBRE" in green text. The background of the image is white.

The image displays a checklist for the launch phase of a product or service, titled "Launch Phase Checklist" in bold black text at the top center. The checklist is divided into two sections: **Pre-Launch Testing** and **Go-Live Deployment**.

**Pre-Launch Testing**

* Submit test form on each landing page
* Verify test campaign delivery email received
* Check automation workflows trigger correctly
* Test ads for issues (test brand)
* View pages on mobile and desktop
* Verify tracking pixels fire correctly
* Check email sequences display properly
* Set up SMS delivery if applicable
* Review all content for typos and errors

**Go-Live Deployment**

* Ads submitted for platform approval
* Ad campaigns scheduled or launched
* Social media posts scheduled
* Blog post published
* Email campaigns activated
* Automation workflows turned ON
* Team notified campaign is live
* Clear info of launch
* Monitoring schedule established

A yellow warning box at the bottom of the image reads, "Never launch without testing - fix issues before they cost you money." The background of the image is white, with a logo in the top-right corner that says "LIBRE" in green text. Above the title, there is a small blue oval with the word "PHASE 3" in white text.

The image displays a slide from a presentation titled "Phase 4: Monitor and Optimize," which outlines an ongoing campaign management checklist for achieving maximum performance. 

**Title and Subtitle**
The title, "Phase 4: Monitor and Optimize," is prominently displayed in bold black text at the top of the image. Below it, in smaller gray text, is the subtitle: "Ongoing campaign management checklist for maximum performance."

**Checklist**
The main content of the slide is organized into three columns, each representing a different time frame: 

* **Daily (First Week)**: High-frequency monitoring during the first week to catch immediate issues and ensure stability.
	+ Daily Checklist:
		- Check for technical issues
		- Monitor ad spend & pacing
		- Respond to inquiries
		- Check ad creative
		- Create form automations
* **Weekly (Ongoing)**: Regular checks for performance analysis, compliance with goals, and technical optimizations.
	+ Weekly Checklist:
		- Optimize ad performance/CPC/ROAS
		- Compare performance to goals
		- Analyze clickstream
		- Review qualification quality
		- Adjust budget allocation
* **Monthly (Strategic)**: Dive deep into data to inform major changes, messaging updates, and campaign reporting.
	+ Monthly Checklist:
		- Conversion analysis
		- Review A/B test variations
		- Optimize campaign strategy
		- Plan next month's strategy

**Additional Information**
At the bottom of the slide, a green dot precedes the text: "Pro Tip: The first week is critical — monitor daily to ensure stability, then shift to a sustainable weekly rhythm." The background of the slide features a white base color with light blue rectangles behind each checklist section. A logo for "LIBRE" is situated in the top-right corner.

The image presents a comprehensive infographic titled "What to Track," which outlines essential metrics for evaluating campaign performance. The title is prominently displayed at the top, accompanied by the subtitle "Your campaign performance dashboard." The infographic is divided into six sections, each highlighting a key metric:

**Metrics and Definitions:**

*   **Reach/Impressions:** 
    *   Definition: How many people see your campaign
    *   Target: Track by channel to see top visibility sources
*   **Click-Through Rate:** 
    *   Definition: Percentage who click to landing page
    *   Benchmark: 2% to 5% is a target for paid ads
*   **Lead Conversion Rate:** 
    *   Definition: Percentage of visitors who become leads
    *   Benchmark: 5% to 10% is a good target for lead magnets
*   **Cost Per Lead:** 
    *   Definition: Total spend divided by leads generated
    *   Target: Must be less than customer value
*   **Lead Quality:** 
    *   Definition: Percentage of qualified leads
    *   Strategy: Use BANT criteria to filter quality
*   **Campaign ROI:** 
    *   Definition: Revenue generated versus cost
    *   Target: Minimum 2x for profitability

**Key Takeaway:**

*   A black button at the bottom of the image reads, "Track these metrics to optimize continuously."

The infographic features a clean design with a white background, green and black text, and colorful lines separating the sections. The Libre logo is displayed in the top-right corner. Overall, this infographic provides a clear and concise overview of the essential metrics for evaluating campaign performance, making it a valuable resource for marketers and campaign analysts.

The image presents a comprehensive guide to avoiding common pitfalls in campaign management, titled "5 Campaign Pitfalls to Avoid." The title is prominently displayed at the top, accompanied by a subtitle that reads, "Learn from common mistakes and understand their business impact."

Below the title, five potential pitfalls are listed on the left side of the image, each represented by a red triangle with an exclamation mark inside. These pitfalls include:

*   **Launching Without Testing**
    *   Impacts: Broken forms, dead links, wasted budget
*   **No Clear Success Criteria**
    *   Impacts: Can't tell if campaign worked/effectiveness
*   **Set It and Forget It**
    *   Impacts: Tremendous downtime, opportunities missed
*   **Ignoring Lead Quality**
    *   Impacts: High volume, low conversions, wasted sales time
*   **Giving Up Too Soon**
    *   Impacts: Quit before optimization delivers results

On the right side of the image, corresponding solutions to these pitfalls are provided, represented by green checkmarks. These solutions include:

*   **Always test everything before going live**
*   **Define specific metrics and targets upfront**
*   **Weekly monitoring minimums, monthly optimization**
*   **Track lead-to-customer rate, adjust targeting**
*   **Give campaigns 30 days minimum, test variations**

At the bottom of the image, a black button with white text reads, "Most campaign failures are preventable with proper process." The background of the image features a clean white design, with the Libre logo displayed in the top-right corner. Overall, the image provides a clear and concise visual guide for campaign managers to avoid common mistakes and ensure the success of their campaigns.

The image presents a concise summary of Lesson 3.6, titled "Key Takeaways," with the subtitle "Summary of Lesson 3.6" in smaller text beneath. The Libre logo, featuring a green plant sprouting from the letter "I," is prominently displayed in the top-right corner.

**Key Takeaways Section**

The left side of the image outlines six key takeaways from the lesson, each accompanied by a green checkmark:

* Campaign success requires systematic planning through 4 phases: Plan, Build, Launch, and Optimize.
* Pre-launch planning involves defining goals, selecting channels, developing messaging, and preparing resources.
* The 4-phase plan covers key tasks: market research, landing pages, setting up automation, and implementing tracking.
* Launch requires thorough testing before deployment, while ongoing monitoring uncovers insights from daily checks to inform strategy.
* Track key metrics such as CTR, conversion rate, cost per lead, test quality, and ROI.
* Avoid common pitfalls like launching without testing, having no success criteria, or quitting too soon.

**Skills Gained Section**

On the right side of the image, a dark blue box with white text highlights the skills gained:

* Systematic campaign planning & execution
* Professional campaign management
* Launch testing & quality assurance
* Optimization from conception to live

**Available Templates Section**

Below the skills gained section, a light green box lists available templates:

* Campaign Planning Checklist
* Launch Readiness Tracker

The background of the image is white, with a green line running along the bottom and left side, creating a clean and visually appealing design. Overall, the image effectively summarizes the key takeaways from Lesson 3.6, providing a clear and concise overview of the important concepts and skills gained.
